name: Quinoa Stuffed Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. Hollow out the insides of the onions using an apple corer, leaving the bottoms intact and reserving the hollowed-out parts.
2. Steam the hollowed-out onions until tender, reserving 3/4 cup of cooking liquid.
3. Rinse and drain the quinoa.
4. In a saucepan, bring 1 cup water and salt to a boil. Stir in the quinoa and return to a boil.
5. Lower the heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
6. Remove from heat and let stand, covered, for 10 minutes.
7. Fluff with a fork.
8. Finely chop the reserved onions.
9. Sauté chopped onions, garlic, mushrooms, and celery in oil for 15 minutes or until soft.
10. Mix in the quinoa and chickpeas and heat through (about 5 minutes).
11. Fill the onions with the quinoa mixture, arranging any excess stuffing on a serving dish around the bottoms of the onions.
12. In a small bowl, crush walnuts using a food processor, adding soy sauce and vinegar to form a creamy mixture. Blend in the reserved cooking liquid.
13. Place the mixture in a saucepan and heat through, stirring constantly.
14. Pour over the stuffed onions, garnish with parsley, and serve.
Per serving: 325 cal; 11 g prot; 195 mg sod; 35 g carb; 17 g fat; 0 mg chol; 164 mg calcium
From the files of DEEANNE
